mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in housing starts signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate within 2 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% decrease in the unemployment rate within 3 months signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in industrial production within 2 months signals a strengthening supply cycle that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate within 2 months and a 1% increase in the consumer price index within 3 months signal a monetary easing cycle that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < -0.5% in 2 months and CPIAUCSL > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% decrease in the unemployment rate within 3 months and a 1% increase in payroll employment within 2 months signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < -0.3% in 3 months and PAYEMS > 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only during expansion regimes.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
